name: Adducted / Internally Rotated Shoulder
description: Shoulder held in adduction and internal rotation, arm pressed against trunk

name: Flexed Elbow
description: Elbow held in flexion, difficulty with extension

name: Pronated Forearm
description: Forearm held in pronation, difficulty supinating

name: Flexed Wrist
description: Wrist held in flexion, may include radial or ulnar deviation

name: Clenched Fist
description: Fingers flexed into palm, difficulty opening hand

name: Thumb-in-Palm
description: Thumb adducted and flexed into palm. Note: refractory cases may also need flexor pollicis brevis and opponens pollicis (not yet in this dataset).

name: Flexed Hip
description: Hip held in flexion, difficulty with extension during gait

name: Adducted Hip (Scissoring)
description: Hips adducted with scissoring gait pattern, legs crossing midline. Note: gracilis and adductor brevis are also standard adductor injection targets (not yet in this dataset).

name: Stiff Knee Gait
description: Knee held in extension during swing phase, difficulty with clearance. Rectus femoris is the primary target (it's biarticular and active in swing-phase knee flexion). Vastus intermedius may contribute as a secondary target. Avoid the other vasti — they stabilize the knee in stance phase and weakening them collapses weight-bearing.

name: Flexed Knee
description: Knee held in flexion, crouch gait pattern

name: Equinus (Plantarflexion)
description: Ankle held in plantarflexion, toe-walking pattern

name: Equinovarus
description: Ankle in plantarflexion and inversion, foot turned inward. Note: tibialis anterior is typically WEAK in this pattern (not spastic) — its weakness is a diagnostic feature; do not inject it.

name: Claw Toes / Curled Toes
description: Toes flexed/clawed, painful with shoe wear

name: Cervical Dystonia / Torticollis
description: Abnormal head/neck positioning — rotation, lateral flexion, or extension. ⚠️ This dataset covers a subset of cervical muscles; splenius capitis (the most common rotational torticollis target), semispinalis capitis, and scalenes are not yet included.
